steps.title: Recognize the experimental logic
steps.content: If CO2 were truly an additional cue to midges, the trap with calls + CO2 would attract significantly more midges than the calls-only trap. The conclusion that CO2 is not a cue requires evidence that adding CO2 makes no difference—or actually reduces attraction.

steps.title: Find the result that proves CO2 is irrelevant
steps.content: Choice C shows more midges in the calls-only trap than in the calls + CO2 trap. This proves that CO2 does not enhance midge attraction and therefore is not serving as an additional cue, directly supporting the conclusion. Choices A, B, and D all suggest CO2 either increases attraction (A, D) or serves as a signal the midges actively respond to (B). That matches choice C — C is correct.
